inspect-unstaged-changes-with-diff.label: Inspect Exact Project Changes
inspect-unstaged-changes-with-diff.summary: Inspect exact supplied text changes without needing web-development knowledge.

sketch2.title: Name the File When Focus Matters

try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.title: Edit and Inspect the Community Heading
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.prompt:
Open `community-site/index.html`. Find the exact line `

Community Events

` and replace it with `

Community Events and Resources

`. Save the file. Then type `git diff -- index.html` and press Enter.
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.hint: Replace only the supplied heading line, then inspect that file.

help.concept: git diff compares the working-tree file with its staged version; the exact edit is supplied because this course assesses Git, not HTML.
help.hint_1: Do not change the title or any other line.
help.hint_2: Inspect the saved change before staging it.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site'

#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site'

try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.instructions:
Open `community-site/index.html`. Find the exact line `

Community Events

` and replace it with `

Community Events and Resources

`. Save the file. Then type `git diff -- index.html` and press Enter.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.title: Edit and Inspect the Footer Spacing
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.prompt: Keep the existing index.html change. Open `community-site/styles.css`. Find `footer { margin-top: 12px; }` and replace it with `footer { margin-top: 24px; }`. Save the file. Then type `git diff -- styles.css` and press Enter.
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.hint: Replace only the supplied footer line, then inspect styles.css.

help.concept: A path-limited diff focuses on one change while another unfinished file remains in the same working tree.
help.hint_1: You do not need to understand CSS; use the exact replacement.
help.hint_2: The index.html change must remain present.

try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.instructions: Keep the existing index.html change. Open `community-site/styles.css`. Find `footer { margin-top: 12px; }` and replace it with `footer { margin-top: 24px; }`. Save the file. Then type `git diff -- styles.css` and press Enter.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.title: Inspect One File While Two Are Changed
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.prompt: Both exact edits now exist in `community-site/`. Type `git diff -- index.html` and press Enter. Confirm the output is limited to the heading change and leaves both files unchanged.
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.hint: Name index.html after `--` to narrow the review.

help.concept: Path-limited inspection does not stage, restore, or modify either file.
help.hint_1: Only index.html should appear in this diff.
help.hint_2: styles.css remains changed for the next topic.

single-choice-1.title: Using git diff
single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Move from a file-level status report to a line-level review, and separate unrelated edits before they reach the staging area.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: After the change has already been committed
options.c: When you want to list earlier commit subjects
options.d: When you want to create repository metadata

multi-choice-2.title: Interpreting Diff Output
multi-choice-2.prompt: Which symbol in `git diff` output indicates a line has been added?
multi-choice-2.hint: Choose the one answer that describes this command or concept precisely.

help.concept: Move from a file-level status report to a line-level review, and separate unrelated edits before they reach the staging area.
help.hint_1: Remove choices that act on a different repository layer or promise behavior Git does not provide.
help.hint_2: Prefer the exact definition used in this chapter over a nearby Git idea.

fill-blank-choice-1.title: Symbol for Removed Lines
fill-blank-choice-1.prompt: In `git diff`, lines that have been removed are indicated by the symbol `[blank1]`.
fill-blank-choice-1.hint: Choose the term or symbol that matches the exact state described, not a nearby Git concept.

help.concept: Move from a file-level status report to a line-level review, and separate unrelated edits before they reach the staging area.
help.hint_1: Pay attention to whether the prompt describes an untracked path, a working-tree edit, staged work, or history.
help.hint_2: Read spaces, punctuation, and status columns as meaningful when the question shows Git output.

fill-blank-choice-1.template: In `git diff`, lines that have been removed are indicated by the symbol `[blank1]`.

fill-blank-choice-2.title: Purpose of git diff
fill-blank-choice-2.prompt: The primary purpose of `git diff` is to show changes in the [blank1] that are not yet staged.
fill-blank-choice-2.hint: Choose the term or symbol that matches the exact state described, not a nearby Git concept.

help.concept: Move from a file-level status report to a line-level review, and separate unrelated edits before they reach the staging area.
help.hint_1: Pay attention to whether the prompt describes an untracked path, a working-tree edit, staged work, or history.
help.hint_2: Read spaces, punctuation, and status columns as meaningful when the question shows Git output.

sketch0.title: Diff Shows Exact Text Changes
sketch0.bodyMarkdown: `git diff -- path` compares a working-tree file with its staged version. Removed lines begin with `-`; added lines begin with `+`. The lesson supplies every HTML, CSS, and text edit, so your task is to inspect the change—not invent code.

sketch2.title: Name the File When Focus Matters
sketch2.bodyMarkdown: When several files differ, add `-- path` to review one file. This keeps the question narrow and does not alter any project state.

sketch3.title: Inspect Before You Stage
sketch3.bodyMarkdown: A diff is the last chance to catch an unintended working-tree edit before it enters the proposed snapshot. The course repeats the inspection habit, not the definition.
